data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Netflix employs a microservices architecture to decouple various functionalities and achieve scalability.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes Docker containers orchestrated by Kubernetes, with a service mesh for communication.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Extensive use of CDNs to deliver content efficiently worldwide.
data.technical_spec: CDNs such as Akamai and Amazon CloudFront are utilized to cache and deliver content.

data.title: Resilient Data Layer
data.description: Netflix employs a resilient data layer to handle massive data traffic and throughput.
data.technical_spec: Uses technologies such as Apache Cassandra and Elastic Search for data storage and retrieval.

data.title: Global Load Balancing
data.description: Distributed global load balancing to manage traffic and ensure uptime.
data.technical_spec: Employs DNS-based load balancing and Regional Secure Gateways.

data.title: Serverless Computing
data.description: Adoption of serverless computing for certain use cases to improve efficiency.
data.technical_spec: AWS Lambda and Google Cloud Functions are leveraged for some operations.
